session_summary: LPC character system Phases 1-3 implemented but two P1 bugs found during user testing
done: Phase 1: LayeredSpriteController, AnimationFrameDriver, BodyPartData, AnimationStateFrameMap, BodyPartSlot enum
Phase 2: CharacterAppearanceSaveData, BodyPartRegistry, SaveManager v6 migration with appearanceData field
Phase 3: AppearanceCustomization step in CharacterCreationController (hair/skin/hair-color cycling), UILayeredSpritePreview
LPCSetupWizard: downloads body/head/hair from GitHub, slices spritesheets, creates assets, updates prefab
Fixed invisible player sprite when no LPC assets exist (fallback to root SpriteRenderer)
Wizard now creates BodyPartRegistry asset and wires to SaveManager
remaining: ProjectNameHere-zl5 (P1 BUG): Wizard only downloads 1 hair style (hair/long/adult). Need 5-6 styles minimum for meaningful character customization
ProjectNameHere-gz3 (P1 BUG): Character creation UI changes/breaks on second run. Likely CreateRuntimeUI lifecycle issue — panels may get double-created or state conflicts between runs
decisions: Frame-index-driven animation: Animator controls timing, AnimationFrameDriver computes frame index from normalizedTime, all layers share same frame
Appearance step is conditional — only shows when BodyPartRegistry has parts (graceful degradation)
SaveManager stores appearance as part IDs + color hex strings, resolved via BodyPartRegistry at load time
Root SpriteRenderer re-enabled as fallback when no layered appearance config exists
next_steps: BUG FIX zl5: Update LPCSetupWizard to download multiple hair styles. Add to BODY_PARTS or make DownloadAndImportSprites download from a list of hair dirs: long, bob, pixie, ponytail, curly_short, buzzcut. Each creates a separate BodyPartData with slot=Hair. Rebuild BodyPartRegistry after.

BUG FIX gz3: Debug CharacterCreationController runtime UI lifecycle. Check MainMenuController lines 231-244 and 406-419 (EnsureCharacterCreation). The appearance panel may not be cleaned up between creation attempts, or CreateRuntimeUI may conflict with existing panels. Test: create char -> cancel -> create again. Likely fix: check for existing panels before building, or properly destroy/reset on cancel.
After fixes: re-run wizard Run All Steps, delete save slot, test full Name->Class->Appearance->Start flow twice
